name: Fitero, Corella y Cervera del Rio Alhama — circular through the Montes de Cierzo
name_original: Fitero, Corella y Cervera del Río Alhama — circular por los Montes de Cierzo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cintruénigo traverse a landscape characterized by fertile fields, gentle hills, and the expansive Ebro Valley. The region features agricultural lands with vineyards and olive groves, alongside natural environments near the Alhama River. This diverse terrain offers a mix of challenging and leisurely routes for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Cintruénigo The most popular road cycling route is Fitero, Corella y Cervera del Rio Alhama — circular through the Montes de Cierzo, a 36.9 miles (59.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cidacos Greenway – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Estación de Cintruénigo, a moderate 53.5 miles (86.2 km) path. This route includes sections along the Cidacos Greenway, providing a mix of river-side and rural scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Fitero – Cidacos River Valley Viewpoint loop from Estación de Cintruénigo, a 74.3 miles (119.5 km) trail leading through the Cidacos River Valley, often completed in about 6 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Cintruénigo is defined by fertile fields, gentle hills, and the Ebro Valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Cintruénigo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cintruénigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cintruénigo?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Cintruénigo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 500 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Cintruénigo?
A: Road cycling routes in Cintruénigo traverse a diverse landscape. You'll find fertile fields, gentle rolling hills, and expansive sections of the Ebro Valley. The region is characterized by agricultural lands with vineyards and olive groves, as well as natural environments near the Alhama River.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Cintruénigo offers routes for various abilities. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy route available. For a moderate option that provides a good introduction to the area's scenery, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990086037/fitero-corella-y-cervera-del-rio-alhama-circular-through-the-montes-de-cierzo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fitero, Corella y Cervera del Rio Alhama — circular through the Montes de Cierzo&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Cintruénigo offers several difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fitero – Cidacos River Valley Viewpoint loop from Estación de Cintruénigo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 75 miles (120 km) and features significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Cintruénigo offers several interesting natural features and landmarks. You can explore the unique rock formations of the nearby Bardenas Reales Natural Park, or cycle along the Alhama River and its sotos. The area also features wetlands and reservoirs like La Estanquilla, La Nava, and La Estanca. For specific points of interest, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument, or the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fitero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Cintruénigo?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cintruénigo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990086037/fitero-corella-y-cervera-del-rio-alhama-circular-through-the-montes-de-cierzo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fitero, Corella y Cervera del Rio Alhama — circular through the Montes de Cierzo&lt;/a&gt; route, which offers a varied journey.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cintruénigo?
A: The southern part of Navarra, including Cintruénigo, generally offers favorable conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for exploring the region's vineyards, olive groves, and riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' road cycling routes are not explicitly highlighted in the data, the presence of easy and moderate routes, particularly those along the Ebro Valley or sections of the Cidacos Greenway, can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with road cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31485883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Estación de Cintruénigo&lt;/a&gt; includes sections along a greenway, which might offer a more relaxed experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cintruénigo?
A: The road cycling routes in Cintruénigo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, from fertile fields to gentle hills, and the scenic beauty of the Ebro Valley and Alhama River areas.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Cintruénigo and the surrounding towns offer amenities for cyclists. The region is known for its local wineries, some of which may offer tours and tastings. The historic town center of Cintruénigo itself has emblazoned palace-houses from the 17th and 18th centuries, suggesting local establishments where you can find refreshments or accommodation.

Q: Are there routes that follow river valleys or greenways?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate river valleys and greenways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31485883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Estación de Cintruénigo&lt;/a&gt; includes sections along the Cidacos Greenway, offering a mix of river-side and rural scenery. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fitero – Cidacos River Valley Viewpoint loop from Estación de Cintruénigo&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Cidacos River Valley.

Q: What historical sites can I visit while road cycling in the area?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore historical sites. The town of Cintruénigo itself has an old quarter with palace-houses from the 17th and 18th centuries. Nearby, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbatial Palace&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a glimpse into the region's rich history.
tips:
Fitero stands out for three main reasons:

Historical and architectural heritage (its Cistercian monastery),
Thermal wellness (a highly regarded spa), and
Outstanding natural beauty (Bardenas Reales Natural Park and hiking and biking trails).

It also boasts local cuisine and a peaceful, welcoming atmosphere, ideal for getting away from it all.

If you're looking for a trip that combines culture, relaxation, and nature in an authentic setting, Fitero is a highly recommended option.
Port of Fitero about 6 km uphill

Cervera del Río Alhama is located on the banks of Cabretón, Aguilar del Río Alhama, Valverde, Fitero, Cintruénigo, on the banks of the Alhama River. It is divided into two neighborhoods by "La Peña", at the top of which are the ruins of the castle.

Corella is a highly recommended destination for:

Lovers of Baroque art who want to enjoy perfectly preserved churches, palaces, and convents.
Nature enthusiasts who enjoy walks along the river, lagoons, and riverside trails.
Those who appreciate the local, authentic culture, alive in festivals and traditions.
Food tourists seeking traditional dishes made with high-quality ingredients.
